title: Complex Clauses and Concision
explain: Focus on clause reduction, relative clauses, and precise connectors. Prefer concise structures without losing meaning.

questions.q: The policy, ___ last year, reduced energy use by 12 percent.
questions.explain: Use a reduced relative clause: 'introduced last year'.

questions.q: The report argues that the findings are robust; ___, it calls for further replication.
questions.explain: The second clause contrasts with the first, so 'however' fits.

questions.q: The lab built a model ___ explains the variance across regions.
questions.explain: A restrictive relative clause for a thing uses 'that' (or 'which').

questions.q: The committee rejected the proposal, ___ its expected benefits.
questions.explain: Use 'despite' + noun phrase. 'Although' would need a clause.

questions.q: The students submitted their drafts, many of ___ were incomplete.
questions.explain: Non‑human antecedent requires 'which'.

questions.q: The survey was expanded ___ more reliable data could be obtained.
questions.explain: Purpose is best expressed with 'so that' + clause.

questions.q: The article is compelling; ___, some claims lack evidence.
questions.explain: Contrast with concession: 'nevertheless'.

questions.q: The team used a larger sample, ___ the original study relied on only 40 participants.
questions.explain: Use 'whereas' to contrast two clauses.

questions.q: The new procedure is cheaper, not ___ more accurate.
questions.explain: The fixed pattern is 'not only ... but also' or 'not even'. Here: 'not even more accurate'.

questions.q: Participants were excluded ___ they had not completed the consent form.
questions.explain: A cause is needed: 'because'.

title: Inversion and Advanced Conditionals
explain: Practice inversion, mixed conditionals, and formal academic structures.

questions.q: ___ the funding been approved, the project would have started in May.
questions.explain: Inversion for past unreal condition: 'Had the funding been approved'.

questions.q: If the data ___ correctly, the model would not have failed.
questions.explain: Past unreal condition requires past perfect: 'had been processed'.

questions.q: Were the results to replicate, the theory ___ stronger support.
questions.explain: Inverted conditional uses 'would' for the result.

questions.q: Not only ___ the response time improve, but errors also declined.
questions.explain: After 'not only' in past tense, use 'did' + base verb.

questions.q: Had the team known about the delay, they ___ the schedule earlier.
questions.explain: Past unreal condition result: 'would have adjusted'.

questions.q: If it were not for the grant, the study ___ possible.
questions.explain: Present unreal condition: 'would not be'.

questions.q: Should the experiment fail again, the team ___ the method.
questions.explain: Inverted first conditional: 'Should ..., will ...'.

questions.q: Only after the revision ___ the article accepted.
questions.explain: Inversion after 'Only after': 'Only after ... was the article accepted'.

questions.q: If she ___ more time, she would have run a second analysis.
questions.explain: Mixed conditional: past time reference with 'had'.

questions.q: Seldom ___ such a comprehensive dataset been published.
questions.explain: Inversion after a negative adverb: 'Seldom has ...'.
